name: Negroni
description: A classic equal-parts aperitif cocktail with bitter, herbal, and spirit-forward character. Equal measures of gin substitute (using your rye for backbone), Campari, and sweet vermouth create a perfectly balanced, warming drink ideal for dinner.
steps: Combine rye, Campari, and sweet vermouth in a mixing glass with ice
Stir for 10–15 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice
Express orange peel oils over the drink and drop as garnish
why: You have every ingredient in your collection. This spirit-forward drink showcases your rye and Campari beautifully, with the vermouth adding depth. Perfect for a dinner party where guests appreciate bold, classic cocktails.

name: Sazerac (Rye Variation)
description: A potent, aromatic spirit-forward drink with rye whiskey, absinthe rinse, and bitters. Historically one of America's finest cocktails, it's bold and sophisticated for a dinner party crowd.
steps: Rinse a rocks glass with absinthe, discard excess
Add sugar and dashes of both bitters to the glass
Add ice and rye whiskey
Stir well for 10–15 seconds
Express lemon peel oils over the drink and drop as garnish
why: Your Rittenhouse Rye is perfect for this iconic spirit-forward cocktail. You'll need to source absinthe and Peychaud's bitters—both are specialty bottles worth having for classic cocktail nights. The result is a warming, complex drink that commands respect at a dinner party.

name: Negroni
description: A classic aperitivo cocktail with equal parts gin substitute, Campari, and sweet vermouth, stirred and served up or over ice. Here we use rye whiskey for a spicier, more bracing take on the traditional formula.
steps: Add rye, Campari, sweet vermouth, and bitters to a mixing glass filled with ice
Stir for 10–15 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a coupe or serve over fresh ice in a rocks glass
Express an orange twist over the drink if available
why: This is the quintessential bitter, bracing aperitivo—Campari's herbal bitterness paired with rye's spice and vermouth's complexity creates a sophisticated, dry-leaning drink that opens the palate without sweetness. Stirred-only and made entirely from your bottles.

name: Hot Toddy
description: A warming, comforting nightcap with bourbon, honey, spices, and hot water, garnished with a fresh orange slice.
steps: Warm a mug with hot water, then discard the water
Add honey and bourbon to the mug
Pour 4-6 oz of hot water into the mug and stir well
Garnish with a cinnamon stick and orange slice
Serve immediately while hot
why: The Hot Toddy is the quintessential warming nightcap—bourbon's vanilla and caramel notes pair beautifully with honey and spice, and the heat makes it perfect for winding down. You have everything needed except the cinnamon, which is a common household spice.

name: Tropical Bourbon Punch
description: A refreshing, fruity tiki-style punch built on bourbon with tropical juices and a touch of sweetness, perfect for a beach party.
steps: Fill a tiki mug or tall glass with crushed ice
Pour Four Roses Small Batch over the ice
Add pineapple juice and orange juice
Stir in simple syrup and fresh lime juice
Stir well to combine
Garnish with a pineapple wedge and cherry if available
why: Four Roses Small Batch has enough vanilla and spice character to anchor a tropical punch without overpowering the fruit flavors. The bourbon's sweetness pairs beautifully with pineapple and orange, creating an approachable tiki drink that feels festive and beachy.

name: Old Fashioned
description: A timeless bourbon cocktail with deep caramel and subtle spice notes, stirred cold with a citrus twist.
steps: Muddle sugar and water in a rocks glass
Add bourbon and stir
Fill glass with ice
Express orange peel oils over the drink and drop in as garnish
why: A perfect showcase for Four Roses' balanced vanilla and floral notes. Requires only pantry staples plus citrus you likely have.

name: Bourbon Smash
description: A refreshing, citrus-forward bourbon drink with muddled mint and a bright, tart finish.
steps: Muddle mint gently in a cocktail shaker
Add bourbon, lemon juice, and simple syrup
Fill shaker with ice and shake vigorously
Strain into a glass filled with fresh ice
Garnish with a mint sprig
why: A crowd-pleasing drink that balances bourbon's warmth with citrus brightness. Works with fresh lemon and mint from most kitchens.

name: Bourbon Neat with a Water Back
description: Four Roses Small Batch enjoyed at its best, with a chaser of cool water to open up the spirit's complexity.
steps: Pour bourbon into a rocks glass
Add ice if desired, or serve at room temperature
Sip the bourbon, then take a water chaser to cleanse the palate and reveal new flavors
why: Sometimes the best surprise is appreciating what you have. Four Roses Small Batch is complex enough to reward slow sipping and reveals different notes as it opens up.

name: Mezcal Negroni
description: A smoky, spirit-forward twist on the classic Negroni, built with mezcal instead of gin. Bitter, complex, and perfectly balanced for a sophisticated date night.
steps: Add mezcal, Campari, and dry vermouth to a mixing glass with ice
Stir well for about 10 seconds until chilled
Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice
Add 2 dashes of Angostura bitters
Express an orange twist over the drink (if available) and serve
why: This drink plays directly to your wife's preferences—mezcal-forward, spirit-driven, and bone-dry with zero added sweetness. The smoke from the mezcal pairs beautifully with Campari's herbal bitterness and the dry vermouth's subtle botanicals. It's intimate, complex, and perfect for a smoky date night vibe.

name: Mezcal Paloma
description: A bright, citrusy tequila-style cocktail built on smoky mezcal with fresh lime juice, grapefruit, and a touch of orange liqueur for depth.
steps: Add mezcal, Cointreau, lime juice, grapefruit juice, and simple syrup to a cocktail shaker filled with ice
Shake vigorously for 10–12 seconds
Strain into a salt-rimmed highball glass filled with fresh ice
Garnish with a lime wheel
why: Mezcal's smoky character pairs beautifully with taco night, while the bright citrus (lime and grapefruit) keeps it refreshing and summery. Cointreau adds a subtle orange note that ties the drink together without overpowering it.

name: Sazerac
description: A classic New Orleans cocktail with rye whiskey, absinthe rinse, and aromatic bitters—bold, warming, and perfect for settling in during a cozy movie night.
steps: Rinse a rocks glass with absinthe (or skip if unavailable) and discard excess
Muddle sugar and water in the glass
Add rye and bitters, stir well
Add ice and stir until chilled
Express lemon peel oil over the drink and drop as garnish
why: The Sazerac is a timeless spirit-forward drink that's warming and contemplative—ideal for movie night. You have all the core ingredients, and it showcases your excellent Rittenhouse Rye. The ritual of making it adds to the cozy atmosphere.

name: Negroni
description: A classic Italian aperitivo cocktail with equal parts gin, Campari, and sweet vermouth, stirred with ice and garnished with an orange twist. Bittersweet, spirit-forward, and perfectly balanced.
steps: Add gin, Campari, and Carpano Antica to a mixing glass filled with ice
Stir for 10–15 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice
Express orange twist over the drink and drop it in as garnish
why: You have two of the three essential ingredients for a true Negroni. You'll need to acquire gin—there's no substitute for this drink. Your Campari and Carpano Antica are exactly right, and the fresh orange you have on hand makes the perfect garnish.

name: Sazerac
description: A bold, spirit-forward New Orleans classic with rye whiskey, absinthe rinse, and aromatic bitters—a true nightcap for serious drinkers.
steps: Rinse a rocks glass with absinthe and discard excess
Add ice to the glass
Pour 2 oz Rittenhouse Rye over ice
Add 2 dashes Angostura Bitters
Stir well for 10–15 seconds
Express lemon peel oils over the drink and drop as garnish
why: You have the two essential bottles for a Sazerac—Rittenhouse Rye and Angostura Bitters. The only missing piece is absinthe for the rinse, which is traditional and irreplaceable in this drink. A lemon peel garnish completes the classic profile.

name: Mai Tai
description: A classic tropical rum cocktail with a perfect balance of sweet, sour, and complex rum flavors, traditionally made with light and dark rum, lime juice, orgeat syrup, and a touch of Curaçao.
steps: Add light rum, dark rum, Curaçao, orgeat syrup, fresh lime juice, and simple syrup to a cocktail shaker filled with ice
Shake vigorously for 10-12 seconds
Strain into a rocks glass filled with crushed ice
Garnish with a lime wheel and mint sprig
why: You requested a Mai Tai specifically. This iconic tropical cocktail is perfect for a beach party, but it requires specialty rums and orgeat syrup that aren't in your current inventory. Your Four Roses bourbon is excellent for other drinks, but a proper Mai Tai demands its traditional rum base.

name: Old Fashioned
description: A timeless bourbon cocktail with deep caramel and spice notes, stirred down with ice and a citrus twist.
steps: Muddle sugar and water in a rocks glass
Add bourbon and stir
Fill glass with ice
Express orange peel oils over the drink and drop in as garnish
why: A perfect showcase for Four Roses' smooth, floral bourbon character. No special ingredients needed—just the classics done right.

name: Sazerac-Style Rye
description: A spicy, herbaceous rye cocktail with anise notes and a dry finish, built on Rittenhouse's bold rye backbone.
steps: Rinse a rocks glass with absinthe and discard excess
Add rye, simple syrup, and bitters to a mixing glass with ice
Stir well and strain into the prepared glass
Express lemon peel and drop in
why: Rittenhouse is perfect for this classic—bold enough to shine through the anise. You'll need absinthe and Peychaud's to complete it properly.

name: Mezcal Margarita
description: A smoky, citrus-forward tequila-style drink with mezcal's mineral complexity and a salt rim.
steps: Rim a coupe or rocks glass with salt
Add mezcal, lime juice, and simple syrup to a shaker with ice
Shake vigorously for 10 seconds
Strain into the prepared glass over fresh ice
why: Del Maguey Vida's bright, vegetal character shines in a margarita format. You just need fresh lime—the mezcal does the heavy lifting.

name: Rye Whiskey Sour
description: A balanced, citrus-forward cocktail with rye's spice and a silky texture from egg white (optional).
steps: Add rye, lemon juice, simple syrup, and egg white to a shaker without ice
Shake dry for 10 seconds (reverse dry shake)
Add ice and shake vigorously for 15 seconds
Strain into a coupe glass
why: Rittenhouse's spicy profile pairs beautifully with bright lemon. The egg white adds silky mouthfeel and a luxe presentation.

name: Mezcal Negroni
description: A smoky, bitter, herbal aperitif with mezcal's mineral notes balanced by vermouth and Campari.
steps: Add mezcal, Campari, and sweet vermouth to a mixing glass with ice
Stir for 30 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice
Express orange peel and drop in as garnish
why: Mezcal's smoke plays beautifully against Campari's bitter-sweet profile. You'll need Campari and vermouth, but the result is sophisticated and surprising.

name: Mezcal Negroni
description: A smoky, spirit-forward aperitif with bitter and herbal notes—a mezcal twist on the classic that leans into smoke and complexity.
steps: Stir mezcal, Campari, dry vermouth, and Angostura bitters with ice for 30 seconds
Strain into a chilled coupe or rocks glass over fresh ice
Express orange peel oils over the drink and use as garnish
why: Plays directly to Alex's love of smoky mezcal and aversion to sweetness. The Campari adds bitterness and herbal depth without cloying sugar. A backyard sipper that rewards slow sipping.

name: Bourbon Manhattan
description: A warming, velvety classic with caramel and vanilla notes—smooth, slightly sweet, and deeply satisfying.
steps: Stir bourbon, sweet vermouth, and Angostura bitters with ice for 30 seconds
Strain into a chilled coupe or rocks glass
Garnish with a cherry
why: Sam's ideal drink—sweet, fruity cherry notes, and the Four Roses brings vanilla and caramel without any bitter edge. Classic, approachable, and perfect for a relaxed backyard setting.

name: Negroni
description: A classic equal-parts aperitivo cocktail balancing bitter Campari, herbal vermouth, and warming bourbon—ideal for aperitivo hour and built for amaro lovers.
steps: Add bourbon, Campari, and sweet vermouth to a mixing glass with ice
Stir for 10–15 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice
Add 2 dashes of Angostura bitters
Express an orange twist over the drink (if available) and serve
why: Jamie loves bitter and amaro—Campari delivers both in spades, while the vermouth adds herbal complexity without cloying sweetness. The bourbon rounds it out with warmth. This is the quintessential aperitivo cocktail.
